title: Memphis Crime Rose 32%, Then Fell 32% — The Property-Crime Wave, Mapped (2020–2026)
titleOptions: 106,788 to 72,843: How Memphis's Property-Crime Wave Broke — 9 Precincts, Mapped
The Memphis Crime Wave Was Property Crime — Watch It Rise and Break, Month by Month
description:
Six years of Memphis's own police record — a property-crime wave that rose 32% to its 2023 crest (106,788 reports) and broke, down 32% by 2025 (72,843) — plus the FBI's arc back to 1985, mapped across all 9 police precincts. The surge and the fall were property crime: crimes against persons stayed in a narrow range throughout.

This is a fully sourced, reproducible data visualization. Every figure comes straight from the FBI Uniform Crime Reporting program and the Memphis Police Department's own open incident data. Every dot on the map is a real reported incident location, published by MPD at block-level (~3-decimal) precision. Coverage and caveats are shown on screen.

The FBI's classic 'index' crimes (through 2019) and MPD's NIBRS Group A records (2020+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, never merged into one line. The step at the seam is the ruler changing, not a crime wave. MPD's dataset omits sex crimes and juvenile-specific types — an MPD publishing decision, disclosed on screen.

▶ CHAPTERS
0:00  Hook — the wave that broke
0:08  What you're about to see
0:22  Chapter 1 — the long arc
2:30  The last five years — up close
2:43  Chapter 2 — the map, month by month
4:52  The busiest & safest areas
5:18  Sources & credits

▶ MADE BY AN AI — FULLY TRANSPARENT
This video was produced end-to-end by Claude (Anthropic's AI): it located and fetched the official source data, built and validated the data pipeline, verified every on-screen figure against the sources, designed and rendered the visuals, and generated the music. No human edited the numbers. Every step — code, data, and design decisions — is public and reproducible in the repository linked below. If you find an error, open an issue; corrections are part of the record.

▶ CODE, DATA & FULL SOURCES (reproduce every number)
https://github.com/hadi-nayebi/crime-cartography/tree/main/videos/memphis-tn

Music generated with Stable Audio Open (Stability AI). Basemap © OpenStreetMap contributors.
